skill.name: design-md
skill.description: Analyze Stitch project screens and create DESIGN.md documentation that captures colors, typography, layout, and component style.
skill.summary: Create semantic DESIGN.md files from Stitch project screens and design assets.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the alleged weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, screen capture upload, and shell execution findings are false positives caused by Markdown design-system text and inline code formatting. The confirmed risk is legitimate network access through Stitch MCP and URL-fetch tools to retrieve project metadata, HTML, and optional screenshots, which requires user awareness but does not show malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Network Access to Stitch Project Assets
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill instructs the agent to use Stitch MCP tools and URL fetch tools to retrieve project metadata, HTML source, and optional screenshots. This is a legitimate capability for design analysis, but it can access private project content and external download URLs, so users should confirm the target project and data scope before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly require Stitch MCP calls and web_fetch or read_url_content for downloaded HTML and optional screenshots. The behavior is visible in the skill text and is necessary for the stated workflow, but no exfiltration endpoint is present.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Markdown Tool References
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The static external command detections occur in Markdown instructions and fenced examples that name MCP tools, fields, and output sections. I did not find shell execution, subprocess creation, or user input passed to an operating system command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain Markdown and tool-call descriptions, not executable Ruby, shell, or process APIs. The repeated detections appear to come from backticks around terms such as list_tools, DESIGN.md, and field names.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The weak cryptography detections appear to match the string MD in DESIGN.md and ordinary design-system prose. I did not find cryptographic functions, hashing APIs, key handling, encryption code, or password storage logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill is a documentation prompt for design systems, and the relevant lines contain Markdown names or design guidance. There is no semantic evidence of cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm use.

low_findings.title: Screen Capture Upload Finding Is Screenshot Analysis Guidance
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The cited screenshot language tells the agent to evaluate a provided or downloaded design screenshot for visual style. I did not find instructions to capture the user screen, upload local screenshots, or send images to an author-controlled service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The screenshot reference is tied to Stitch screen metadata and optional visual analysis. The text does not request operating system screen capture or unauthorized upload behavior.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are Design-System Language
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The system reconnaissance detections occur in phrases about design systems, layout principles, and common documentation pitfalls. I did not find host discovery, environment enumeration, credential discovery, or command-based reconnaissance instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The word system is used in the design-system sense, not the operating-system sense. No commands or semantic recon workflow are present in the cited text.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent design language when extending Stitch projects. This skill turns existing screens, metadata, and visual assets into a structured DESIGN.md reference.
actual_capabilities: Discovers Stitch MCP tools and uses them to locate projects and screens.
Retrieves screen metadata, HTML source links, screenshot links, and design theme fields.
Extracts colors, typography, spacing, shapes, shadows, and component patterns from Stitch assets.
Translates technical Tailwind and CSS values into designer-friendly descriptions.
Creates a structured DESIGN.md with project identity, palette roles, typography, components, and layout principles.
References Stitch prompting guidance when shaping design-system language.
limitations: Requires access to a configured Stitch MCP Server and the target Stitch project.
May fetch private project HTML or screenshots when the user authorizes the workflow.
Does not create new UI screens directly; it produces design documentation.
Accuracy depends on the quality and completeness of the selected source screens.

use_cases.title: Document a New Stitch Project
use_cases.description: Create a DESIGN.md that records the visual theme, palette, typography, components, and layout rules for a project.

use_cases.title: Prepare Consistent Feature Prompts
use_cases.description: Use the generated design reference to prompt future screens that match the existing Stitch design language.

use_cases.title: Review Design System Drift
use_cases.description: Compare screen assets and metadata to identify recurring colors, spacing, shape rules, and component styling patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic DESIGN.md
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow with a known project and screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the design-md skill for my Stitch project. I have project ID [project ID] and screen ID [screen ID]. Create a DESIGN.md that documents the visual theme, colors, typography, components, and layout principles.

prompt_templates.title: Find Project by Name
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a project title but not the internal IDs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Stitch MCP tools to find my project named [project name]. Then analyze the main screen and create a DESIGN.md that can guide future Stitch prompts.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Multiple Screens
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for a broader design reference.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these Stitch screens: [screen names or IDs]. Create one DESIGN.md that captures shared design rules and calls out any meaningful differences between screens.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Design Language for Reuse
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DESIGN.md for [project name] with precise hex colors, semantic color roles, typography rules, component styling, layout principles, and concise language suitable for future Stitch generation prompts.

output_examples.input: A Stitch landing page with soft shadows, rounded cards, blue action buttons, and a spacious grid layout.
output_examples.output: A DESIGN.md section that names the atmosphere, assigns the blue to primary actions, describes cards as softly elevated containers, and records grid spacing rules.

output_examples.input: A mobile screen with neutral backgrounds, compact forms, square inputs, and one accent color.
output_examples.output: A design reference that documents the restrained palette, form field shape, accent usage, mobile density, and interaction hierarchy.

output_examples.input: A project with several screens using similar colors but inconsistent button styles.
output_examples.output: A consolidated DESIGN.md that captures the shared palette and recommends one consistent button shape, color role, and elevation pattern.

best_practices: Provide the project ID and screen ID when available to reduce unnecessary project listing.
Review fetched HTML and screenshot sources before using private or sensitive project data.
Use several representative screens when the project has multiple layouts or states.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill without confirming which Stitch project should be analyzed.
Do not treat generated design language as final without checking visual accuracy.
Do not omit exact color values when the source assets provide them.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a DESIGN.md file that documents a Stitch project design system in clear, reusable language.

faq.question: Does it need network access?
faq.answer: Yes. It may use Stitch MCP tools and URL fetch tools to retrieve project metadata, HTML, and optional screenshots.

faq.question: Can it work without a Stitch project ID?
faq.answer: Yes, if the Stitch MCP Server can list projects and the user provides enough project context to identify the target.

faq.question: Does it generate production code?
faq.answer: No. It analyzes design assets and produces Markdown documentation for future design and prompting work.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing the output?
faq.answer: Users should verify color values, component descriptions, layout rules, and any references to private project details.

faq.question: Is the skill blocked for security reasons?
faq.answer: No. The confirmed risk is network retrieval of project assets, which is expected for the workflow and should be used with consent.
